core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat Agent_5 because the first turn made Apple a non-negotiable anchor and shifted bargaining to non-Apple swaps; everyone later proposed and voted for the same allocation. The result also reflects preference structure: Agent_5, despite speaking last, kept Pencil, its own top item, so the evidence is an anchoring advantage rather than pure order determinism.
why_or_counterexample: Agent_1 used the opening move to assert Apple and the default split before alternatives appeared, and other agents repeatedly accepted Apple as the settled anchor. The counterexample is that Agent_5 also defended and kept Pencil, so the advantage is tied to first-frame anchoring plus Agent_1's very high Apple value, not simply last-position weakness.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened with Apple as non-negotiable and a full default allocation. Later speakers accepted Apple with Agent_1 and focused contention on Stone/Jewel/Pencil while protecting Quill and Pencil.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private reasoning across agents describes Apple as an anchor or uncontested anchor, with the tactical path focused on locking Stone/Jewel and postponing any Pencil compensation discussion.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: All five submitted the same allocation: Agent_1 gets Apple, Agent_2 Jewel, Agent_3 Stone, Agent_4 Quill, Agent_5 Pencil. Proposal reasoning repeatedly says this preserves the Apple anchor.
phase_notes.private_voting: Every private vote accepted every proposal. Agent_1 accepted because Apple gave 68 utility; other agents accepted because the identical allocation secured their own focal items or gains.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection section is present in the transcript.

mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1's first public move made Apple non-negotiable and supplied a complete baseline allocation, giving later agents a frame to accept or modify only around non-Apple items.

evidence.quote: Apple is my top item and I am not willing to part with it

mechanisms.theme: Later speakers ratified the first frame
mechanisms.explanation: After Agent_1's opening, Agents 2 through 5 repeatedly treated Apple with Agent_1 as already acceptable, so their bargaining energy moved elsewhere.

evidence.quote: I support Apple staying with Agent_1

mechanisms.theme: Contest shifted away from Agent_1's item
mechanisms.explanation: The live negotiation narrowed to a Stone/Jewel swap and Pencil compensation, leaving Agent_1's Apple out of the trade space.

evidence.quote: Stone goes to Agent_3; Jewel goes to Agent_2

mechanisms.explanation: The proposal phase converted the public anchor into unanimous identical proposals, all assigning Apple to Agent_1 and producing consensus in one round.

evidence.quote: Apple remains with Agent_1 as the anchor

mechanisms.theme: Private reasoning treated Apple as settled
mechanisms.explanation: Raw private thinking and votes confirm that agents internally viewed Apple as fixed for Agent_1, and Agent_1 saw acceptance as immediately optimal.

evidence.quote: Apple is clearly the non-negotiable anchor
Apple is clearly the uncontested anchor
Apple remains with me and yields 68 utility

mechanisms.theme: Last speaker was not simply dominated
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_5, despite being last, successfully held Pencil, its top item, and accepted proposals because the final allocation maximized its immediate utility. This weakens any claim that the numeric gap is caused only by speaking order.

evidence.quote: preserves Pencil with the agent who values it most
